name: join
description: The join utility performs an 'equality join' on the specified files and writes the result to the standard output. The 'join field' is the field in each file by which the files are compared. The first

options.description: In addition to the default output, produce a line for each unpairable line in file file_number
Replace empty output fields with string
The -o option specifies the fields that will be output from each file for each line with matching join fields.  Each element of list has either the form file_number.field, where file_number is a file
Use character char as a field delimiter for both input and output. Every occurrence of char in a line is significant
Do not display the default output, but display a line for each unpairable line in file file_number. The options -v 1 and -v 2 may be specified at the same time
Join on the field'th field of file1
Join on the field'th field of file2
Join on the field'th field of both file1 and file2

args.name: file1
args.description: First file in the join operation

args.name: file2
args.description: Second file in the join operation
